req.title: Account close (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall close an account with zero balance; closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-089 defines four distinct behavioural rules: (1) zero-balance account closes cleanly, (2) non-zero balance without sweep yields 409, (3) non-zero balance with sweep closes cleanly, and (4) duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key returns the original response. The violation in the previous response was test[5]: it was marked kind=negative but heuristic=STC, which is invalid — STC must pair with positive or boundary kind because it traces valid state-machine transitions. The fix is to change that test's heuristic from STC to NEG, which is the correct heuristic for a negative test exercising an invalid state transition (closed→closed with a new key). All other tests are preserved unchanged.

tests.name: test_close_zero_balance_account_succeeds
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as account owner
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with valid Idempotency-Key and no sweep body
expected: Final statement document reference present in response
trace: R-FIN-089: final statement generated on close
tests.executable:
def test_close_zero_balance_account_succeeds(api_client, zero_balance_account):
    account_id = zero_balance_account["id"]
    idem_key = f"test-close-zero-{account_id}"
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
        json={},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 204), resp.text
    if resp.status_code == 200:
        body = resp.json()
        assert body["state"] == "closed"
        assert "final_statement" in body or "statement_ref" in body

tests.name: test_close_nonzero_balance_without_sweep_returns_409
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as account owner
expected: Error body contains code indicating non-zero balance rejection
trace: R-FIN-089: error pathway — 409 when balance != 0 without sweep
tests.executable:
def test_close_nonzero_balance_without_sweep_returns_409(api_client, nonzero_balance_account):
    account_id = nonzero_balance_account["id"]
    idem_key = f"test-close-nonzero-no-sweep-{account_id}"
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
        json={},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "code" in body
    status_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert status_resp.json()["state"] == "open"

tests.name: test_close_account_with_balance_at_exact_zero_boundary
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as account owner
Account balance is exactly 0.00 (boundary value between zero and non-zero)
expected: HTTP 200 or 204 returned (0.00 is treated as zero, not non-zero)
trace: R-FIN-089: BVA at balance == 0.00 — must succeed without sweep
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_with_balance_at_exact_zero_boundary(api_client, create_account_with_balance):
    account = create_account_with_balance(balance="0.00")
    account_id = account["id"]
    idem_key = f"test-close-bva-zero-{account_id}"
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
        json={},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 204), (
        f"Expected success at 0.00 balance, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    if resp.status_code == 200:
        assert resp.json()["state"] == "closed"

tests.name: test_close_with_nonzero_balance_and_sweep_succeeds
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as account owner
Sweep destination account exists, owned by same caller
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with sweep_to_account_id pointing to destination
trace: R-FIN-089: non-zero balance close with sweep — positive DT cell
tests.executable:
def test_close_with_nonzero_balance_and_sweep_succeeds(api_client, nonzero_balance_account, sweep_destination_account):
    src_id = nonzero_balance_account["id"]
    dst_id = sweep_destination_account["id"]
    prior_bal = sweep_destination_account["balance"]
    idem_key = f"test-close-sweep-{src_id}"
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{src_id}/close",
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
        json={"sweep_to_account_id": dst_id},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 204), resp.text
    dst_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{dst_id}")
    new_bal = float(dst_resp.json()["balance"])
    assert new_bal > float(prior_bal)
    src_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src_id}")
    assert src_resp.json()["state"] == "closed"

tests.name: test_idempotent_close_returns_original_result
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as account owner
A unique Idempotency-Key is reserved for this test run
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with Idempotency-Key X — first submission
POST /accounts/{id}/close with same Idempotency-Key X — duplicate submission
expected: Both requests return the same HTTP status code
Both response bodies are identical (or semantically equivalent)
Account state is 'closed' after both calls
trace: R-FIN-089: idempotency — duplicate Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
def test_idempotent_close_returns_original_result(api_client, zero_balance_account):
    account_id = zero_balance_account["id"]
    idem_key = f"test-idempotent-close-{account_id}"
    first = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
        json={},
    )
    assert first.status_code in (200, 204), first.text
    second = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
        json={},
    )
    assert second.status_code == first.status_code
    if first.status_code == 200:
        assert second.json() == first.json()
    acct = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}").json()
    assert acct["state"] == "closed"

tests.name: test_close_already_closed_account_without_idempotency_key_returns_error
preconditions: Account exists and state is already 'closed'
Request carries a NEW Idempotency-Key (not the one used to close it)
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with a fresh Idempotency-Key on an already-closed account
expected: Error body indicates account is already closed or invalid state transition
trace: R-FIN-089: NEG — invalid transition closed→closed with new key is rejected
tests.executable:
def test_close_already_closed_account_without_idempotency_key_returns_error(api_client, closed_account):
    account_id = closed_account["id"]
    fresh_idem_key = f"test-close-already-closed-fresh-{account_id}"
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": fresh_idem_key},
        json={},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (409, 422), (
        f"Expected 409 or 422 for already-closed account, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "code" in body

I derive test cases using EP (normal close path), NEG (non-zero without sweep), BVA (balance exactly at zero boundary), STC (account state machine: open→closed→closed idempotent), and DT (sweep presence vs absence as a decision variable). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down accounts, and uses a fixed Idempotency-Key seeded with the test name to remain deterministic across runs.",
